page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier single-core results, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 2292.0 and strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations are significantly constrained. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated poor, and the Passmark memory score of 961.1 reflects low memory bandwidth, with uncached read and write speeds limited to 20.28 GB/sec and 14.17 GB/sec respectively.

This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. While the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent single-threaded execution, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak multi-core scaling represent clear tradeoffs. The instance is suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale language model text generation, but is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, parallelized software compilation, or high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded decompression benchmarks, its multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. With no local storage or bundled GPUs, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for single-threaded compute tasks, text processing, and lightweight text generation workloads, though it is constrained for memory-intensive or highly parallelized operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, it offers strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a 2-vCPU compute-optimized instance with 4 GB RAM, featuring an Intel Xeon CPU and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute node featuring advanced Intel instruction sets and strong single-threaded performance for lightweight processing workloads.

Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark results
